note: Company-identifying tokens that must never appear in COMMITTED example / seed / demo / test data. Tokens are stored as lowercase SHA-256 hashes (`token_hashes`) so this public config never itself discloses a name it exists to keep out: the scanner hashes every word-shaped and host-shaped candidate in each committed file and fails on a match. Word semantics are boundary-safe (a short brand token never trips on a longer word containing it); a host-shaped banned token also covers emails at that host. The optional plaintext `tokens` array is still honored for forks that prefer readable lists. Real company data — your competitors, segments, customers, product names — belongs ONLY in your gitignored workspace (~/.claude/escc/...), never here. When you fork ESCC for internal use, add sha256(lowercase(your-org-name)) to token_hashes (node -e "console.log(require('crypto').createHash('sha256').update('yourco').digest('hex'))"). Author/owner names (the MIT LICENSE/plugin.json author) are NOT company tokens and are not listed. CI: scripts/ci/validate-no-company-tokens.js.
